docs.title: Steam Client
docs.text:
Steam Client is a Python library for interacting with a local Steam installation.

Use this site to quickly get started, see common usage patterns, and browse API docs generated from real source modules in this repository.

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text:
• Read installed library folders and games
  • Access game metadata and artwork paths
  • Read local users and non-Steam shortcuts
  • Launch Steam URI commands

docs.title: Project links
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/garulf/steam-client
  • PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/steam-client/

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install steam-client

docs.title: First script
docs.text:
from steam_client.steam import Steam

steam = Steam()
print(steam.library_folders_file)

On Windows, you can use registry discovery:

from steam_client.utils import steam_from_registry

steam = steam_from_registry()
print(steam.app_cache)

docs.title: List games
docs.text:
from steam_client.steam import Steam

steam = Steam()
for game in steam.library.games():
    print(game.appid, game.name)

docs.title: Find a game
docs.text:
game = steam.library.game_by_id("440")
if game:
    print(game.name)

game = steam.library.game_by_name("Portal 2")
if game:
    print(game.appid)

docs.title: Use Steam URI commands
docs.text:
from steam_client import commands
from steam_client.commands import SteamWindow

commands.run_game_id("440")
commands.open_window(SteamWindow.FRIENDS)

docs.title: Read users and shortcuts
docs.text:
for user in steam.users:
    print(user.user.data.PersonaName)

for shortcut in steam.library.shortcuts():
    print(shortcut.name)

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
The API is organized into logical groups. Each page is generated directly from the source modules in this repository.

Module Description Steam Main entry point — wraps a local Steam installation Library Read installed games and non-Steam shortcuts Games & Apps Game, Shortcut, and the shared App base class Commands Send Steam URI commands to the local client Users Read logged-in Steam users Utilities Helper functions and exceptions (VDF parsing, Windows registry discovery)

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
The commands module builds and dispatches steam:// URI commands to the local Steam client via webbrowser.open. SteamWindow is a StrEnum of all openable Steam windows.

from steam_client import commands
from steam_client.commands import SteamWindow

commands.run_game_id("440")
commands.open_window(SteamWindow.FRIENDS)

docs.title: SteamWindow
docs.text:
Bases: StrEnum

Enumeration of Steam client windows.

docs.title: run_game_id
docs.text:
run_game_id(app_id: str) -> None

Launches game with the specified ID in the Steam client.

Source code in steam_client/commands.py 
def run_game_id(app_id: str) -> None:
    """Launches game with the specified ID in the Steam client."""
    _open('rungameid', app_id)

docs.title: Game
docs.text:
Represents an installed Steam game read from an appmanifest_*.acf file.

game = steam.library.game_by_id("440")
print(game.name, game.header)

docs.title: Shortcut
docs.text:
Represents a non-Steam game shortcut. App IDs are computed using the same CRC32 algorithm Steam uses internally.

for shortcut in steam.library.shortcuts():
    print(shortcut.name, shortcut.grid)

docs.title: Library
docs.text:
Library provides access to installed games and non-Steam shortcuts across all Steam library folders on the machine.

for game in steam.library.games():
    print(game.appid, game.name)

docs.title: Library
docs.text:
Library(steam: Steam)

Represents the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def __init__(self, steam: Steam):
    self._steam = steam
    self._libraries_hash: str | None = None
    self._libraries: list[LibraryFolder] = []

docs.title: libraries
docs.text:
libraries() -> Iterator[LibraryFolder]

Yields the Steam library folders, reparsing the file only when it changes.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def libraries(self) -> Iterator[LibraryFolder]:
    """Yields the Steam library folders, reparsing the file only when it changes."""
    try:
        current_hash = self._hash_library_folders_file()
    except FileNotFoundError as error:
        raise SteamNotFoundError(
            f'No Steam library found at {self._steam.base_path}'
        ) from error
    if current_hash != self._libraries_hash:
        self._libraries = self._load_library_folders()
        self._libraries_hash = current_hash
    yield from self._libraries

docs.title: games
docs.text:
games() -> Iterator[Game]

Yields the games from the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def games(self) -> Iterator[Game]:
    """Yields the games from the Steam library."""
    for library in self.libraries():
        yield from library.games()

docs.title: game_by_id
docs.text:
game_by_id(appid: str) -> Game | None

Returns the game with the specified ID.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def game_by_id(self, appid: str) -> Game | None:
    """Returns the game with the specified ID."""
    return next((game for game in self.games() if game.appid == appid), None)

docs.title: game_by_name
docs.text:
game_by_name(name: str) -> Game | None

Returns the game with the specified name (case-insensitive).

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def game_by_name(self, name: str) -> Game | None:
    """Returns the game with the specified name (case-insensitive)."""
    return next(
        (game for game in self.games() if game.name.casefold() == name.casefold()),
        None,
    )

docs.title: shortcuts
docs.text:
shortcuts() -> Iterator[Shortcut]

Yields the Non-Steam shortcuts from the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def shortcuts(self) -> Iterator[Shortcut]:
    """Yields the Non-Steam shortcuts from the Steam library."""
    for user in self._steam.users:
        yield from user.shortcuts()

docs.title: all_apps
docs.text:
all_apps() -> Iterator[Game | Shortcut]

Yields all the games and shortcuts from the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def all_apps(self) -> Iterator[Game | Shortcut]:
    """Yields all the games and shortcuts from the Steam library."""
    yield from self.games()
    yield from self.shortcuts()

docs.title: LibraryFolder
docs.text: A single Steam library folder on disk. Returned by Library.libraries().

Represents a Steam library folder.

docs.title: games
docs.text:
games() -> list[Game]

Returns the games installed in this library folder.

Source code in steam_client/library_folder.py 
def games(self) -> list[Game]:
    """Returns the games installed in this library folder."""
    return [Game(self.library_cache_path, self.path, appid) for appid in self.apps]

docs.title: Steam
docs.text:
The Steam class is the main entry point for the library. Instantiate it with a path to a local Steam installation, or use steam_from_registry() on Windows to auto-detect the path.

from steam_client import Steam

steam = Steam()

docs.title: Users
docs.text:
Provides access to previously logged-in Steam accounts read from loginusers.vdf.

for user in steam.users:
    print(user.user.data.PersonaName, user.is_most_recent)

docs.title: UserData dataclass
docs.text:
UserData(AccountName: str = '', PersonaName: str = '', RememberPassword: str = '0', WantsOfflineMode: str = '0', SkipOfflineModeWarning: str = '0', AllowAutoLogin: str = '0', MostRecent: str = '0', Timestamp: str = '0')

User fields mirrored from loginusers.vdf (names keep Valve's casing).

docs.title: from_vdf classmethod
docs.text:
from_vdf(data: dict[str, Any]) -> UserData

Builds UserData from a raw loginusers.vdf entry, ignoring unknown keys.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
@classmethod
def from_vdf(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> UserData:
    """Builds UserData from a raw loginusers.vdf entry, ignoring unknown keys."""
    known_fields = {field.name for field in fields(cls)}
    return cls(**{key: value for key, value in data.items() if key in known_fields})

docs.title: shortcuts
docs.text:
shortcuts() -> list[Shortcut]

Returns the user's Non-Steam shortcuts; empty if none have been created.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def shortcuts(self) -> list[Shortcut]:
    """Returns the user's Non-Steam shortcuts; empty if none have been created."""
    try:
        data = load_binary_vdf(self.shortcuts_file)
    except FileNotFoundError:
        return []
    return [Shortcut(self, entry) for entry in data['shortcuts'].values()]

docs.title: LoginUsers
docs.text:
LoginUsers(base_path: str | Path)

Represents the loginusers.vdf file.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def __init__(self, base_path: str | Path):
    self._base_path = Path(base_path)

docs.title: users
docs.text:
users() -> list[LoginUser]

Returns the users from the loginusers.vdf file.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def users(self) -> list[LoginUser]:
    """Returns the users from the loginusers.vdf file."""
    try:
        login_users = load_vdf(self._path)
    except FileNotFoundError as error:
        raise SteamNotFoundError(
            f'No Steam login data found at {self._base_path}'
        ) from error
    return [
        LoginUser(self._base_path, User(int(user_id), UserData.from_vdf(user_data)))
        for user_id, user_data in login_users['users'].items()
    ]

docs.title: most_recent_user
docs.text:
most_recent_user() -> LoginUser | None

Returns the most recently logged in user.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def most_recent_user(self) -> LoginUser | None:
    """Returns the most recently logged in user."""
    return next((user for user in self.users() if user.is_most_recent), None)

docs.title: Utilities
docs.text:
Helper functions for platform-specific setup and VDF file parsing.

Windows only

steam_from_registry() and steam_install_path_from_registry() read from the Windows registry and raise OSError on other platforms.

from steam_client.utils import steam_from_registry

steam = steam_from_registry()

docs.title: steam_install_path_from_registry
docs.text:
steam_install_path_from_registry() -> str

Returns the Steam install path from the Windows registry.

Source code in steam_client/utils.py 
def steam_install_path_from_registry() -> str:
    """Returns the Steam install path from the Windows registry."""
    if sys.platform != "win32":
        raise OSError("Windows registry is only available on Windows")

    reg = importlib.import_module("winreg")
    with reg.OpenKey(reg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE, STEAM_SUB_KEY) as hkey:
        return reg.QueryValueEx(hkey, "InstallPath")[0]

docs.title: steam_from_registry
docs.text:
steam_from_registry() -> Steam

Returns a Steam instance located via the Windows registry.

Source code in steam_client/utils.py 
def steam_from_registry() -> Steam:
    """Returns a Steam instance located via the Windows registry."""
    return Steam(steam_install_path_from_registry())

docs.title: VDF files
docs.text: Loaders for Valve's VDF data format, used by all modules that read Steam data files.

docs.title: load_vdf
docs.text:
load_vdf(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]

Returns the parsed contents of a text VDF file.

Source code in steam_client/vdf_file.py 
def load_vdf(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Returns the parsed contents of a text VDF file."""
    with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
        return vdf.load(f)

docs.title: load_binary_vdf
docs.text:
load_binary_vdf(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]

Returns the parsed contents of a binary VDF file.

Source code in steam_client/vdf_file.py 
def load_binary_vdf(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Returns the parsed contents of a binary VDF file."""
    with open(path, 'rb') as f:
        return vdf.binary_load(f)

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text: SteamNotFoundError is raised when a required Steam data file is missing — usually a sign that Steam is not installed at the given base path. It subclasses FileNotFoundError.

docs.title: SteamNotFoundError
docs.text:
Bases: FileNotFoundError

Raised when a required Steam data file is missing.

This usually means Steam is not installed at the given base path.
